_meta.description: Evidence-first, non-accusatory documentation of the City of London Corporation across two tracks: (1) its data-linkage/data-sharing footprint (City of London Police, NFIB, Action Fraud -> Report Fraud); (2) its governance & transparency (City's Cash, the business-vote franchise, the Remembrancer, FOI coverage). Status-graded; gaps become FOI requests.
_meta.note: Sources flagged BLOCKED? returned HTTP 403 to the WebFetch client used here; the source-checker (browser UA) may still reach them. Claims resting only on a BLOCKED?/LIVE-but-unread source are graded LIKELY, not CONFIRMED, until the page text is read in a browser.

unverified_dropped.title: City of London Police — National Lead Force
unverified_dropped.reason: HTTP 403 to every client here (WebFetch + checker). Could not verify resolution, so not recorded. T1-01 is carried by S01 (verified).

unverified_dropped.title: City of London Police — written evidence to Parliament
unverified_dropped.reason: HTTP 403 to checker. Not recorded; T1-01 carried by S01.

unverified_dropped.title: City of London Corporation — FOI requests (WhatDoTheyKnow)
unverified_dropped.reason: HTTP 403 to checker. Not recorded; useful pointer for City's Cash FOI refusals but verify in a browser. T2-05 carried by S05 (live).

links.title: National Fraud Intelligence Bureau
links.note: Fetched & read 2026-06-10. Confirms CoLP runs NFIB (national lead force, Home Office funded); automated analysis/linking of reports into intelligence packages; Action Fraud (2013) -> Report Fraud 4 Dec 2025; 2019 Times + 2018 Which? criticism. Does NOT name Palantir/Microsoft.

links.title: City of London Corporation
links.note: Fetched & read 2026-06-10. Business-vote franchise (abolished elsewhere 1969; ~24,000 business voters by 2009); City's Cash ~£1.3bn (2012 disclosure) / £2.3bn net assets (Mar 2016); Remembrancer handles legislative matters.

links.title: Report Fraud — Privacy information
links.note: URL verified LIVE (HTTP 200) by source-checker 2026-06-10; blocks WebFetch so full page text not personally read here — claims kept at LIKELY. WebFetch 403 here; search index shows: CoLP is data controller; lists Report Fraud Crime Management / Data Migration / Intelligence / Prevention / Victim Services DPIAs with published summaries; DPO data.protection@cityoflondon.police.uk. Read in a browser to confirm.

links.title: Freedom of Information Act 2000 (City of London)
links.note: URL verified LIVE (HTTP 200) by source-checker 2026-06-10; blocks WebFetch so full page text not personally read here — claims kept at LIKELY. WebFetch 403; search index indicates FOIA applies to public functions; City's Cash / privately-funded activities stated outside FOI. Read in browser to confirm exact wording.

links.title: Remembrancer's Office — Working with Parliament
links.note: URL verified LIVE (HTTP 200) by source-checker 2026-06-10; blocks WebFetch so full page text not personally read here — claims kept at LIKELY. WebFetch 403; the Corporation's own description of the Remembrancer's parliamentary-engagement role.

links.title: Business/worker vote registration
links.note: URL verified LIVE (HTTP 200) by source-checker 2026-06-10; blocks WebFetch so full page text not personally read here — claims kept at LIKELY. WebFetch 403; the Corporation's own business-vote registration page (franchise mechanics).

links.title: Streets paved with gold: the council that works for banks
links.note: Investigative secondary source on the Corporation/Remembrancer/City's Cash; dated 2012 — treat as historical context, verify current facts against primary.

links.title: London Police selects Capita and PwC for Action Fraud upgrade work
links.note: Fetched & read 2026-06-11. Names suppliers for the Action Fraud replacement / NFIB tech: Capita (£50m, victim/customer management); PwC (tech for crime & intelligence management underpinning NFIB); and PALANTIR as PwC's 'technology alliance partner' delivering that technology. SINGLE trade-press source — grade LIKELY; corroborate with PwC/CoLP primary or procurement record.

links.title: Palantir, the controversy, the contracts and the campaign against the FDP
links.note: Context for Palantir's broader UK footprint (NHS FDP £330m etc.). Secondary/advocacy source — use for orientation; cite primaries (contract notices, parliamentary records) for any published claim.

links.title: Palantir and PwC UK sign multi-year deal as preferred partners
links.note: Corroborates that PwC+Palantir are a FORMAL UK alliance (preferred partners) — but this is a GENERAL AI-transformation deal, NOT NFIB-specific. WebFetch timed out (not read here); checker to verify liveness. Strengthens plausibility of the NFIB link; does not by itself confirm Palantir on the NFIB contract.
